candidate_check.description: RHEL 8 must use reverse path filtering on all IPv4 interfaces.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify RHEL 8 uses reverse path filtering on IPv4 interfaces with the following commands:

$ sudo sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter

net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1

If "net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter" is not set to "1" or is missing, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure RHEL 8 to use reverse path filtering on IPv4 interfaces by default.

Add or edit the following line in a single system configuration file in the "/etc/sysctl.d/" directory:

Create a configuration file if it does not already exist:

$ sudo vi /etc/sysctl.d/ipv4_rp_filter.conf

net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1

Load settings from all system configuration files with the following command:

$ sudo sysctl --system
title: RHEL 8 must use reverse path filtering on all IPv4 interfaces.
